test_case_resolutions.title: Verify user can login with valid credentials
test_case_resolutions.resolution_summary: This login test case requires significant new component creation. The AURA framework does not have a dedicated standalone login page object or login helper. Authentication is handled through HPIDHelper which focuses on HP ID account creation (sign-up) and sign-in during enrollment flows, not standalone login testing. The test requires creating new page objects for login page and dashboard verification, new locators for login form elements and dashboard elements, and new methods for login operations and logout functionality. The framework provides foundation infrastructure (browser management, base page objects, test setup) but lacks the specific login testing components.

relevant_files.reason: Base class for all page objects. Provides common page interaction methods including waits, element interactions, and navigation helpers. Required because any page object (LoginPage, DashboardPage) must inherit from BasePageObject.

HP ID authentication page object. Contains locators for email, password, and sign-in button elements. While this is primarily for HP ID account creation (sign-up), it may contain some sign-in form elements that could be referenced for the login test. However, KB confirms this is NOT a dedicated login page - it handles HP ID flows during enrollment.

HP ID authentication helper. Provides sign_in(page, email, password) method for existing user authentication and sign_up for new account creation. This is the closest existing component to login functionality, though it's designed for HP ID flows during enrollment rather than standalone login testing.

Dashboard/Overview page object. This is the main dashboard page users see after successful login/enrollment. Contains locators for status cards, plan details, and navigation elements. Needed for verifying successful login by checking dashboard page is displayed. KB confirms this is a GOD NODE with 160 edges indicating high connectivity.

Dashboard side navigation menu page object. Contains navigation links for dashboard sections. May contain locators for navigation menu items and potentially logout button. Needed for verifying navigation menu is displayed after login.
Privacy banner/cookie consent page object. May appear before or after login. Provides methods to accept privacy banner and cookies. Needed to handle potential privacy consent popup during login flow.

Dashboard access and navigation helper. Provides methods for navigating to dashboard and handling first-time access. May be useful for post-login dashboard verification. However, KB does not confirm logout methods exist in this helper.

Foundation test utilities module. Provides test setup, email generation for test accounts, HPID account creation, and screenshot utilities. Required for test initialization and test data generation. Note: This is a MODULE not a class.
Browser lifecycle management. Creates and manages browser instances and page objects. Required for all tests to initialize browser and page for test execution.
Configuration management. Provides environment settings, base URLs, timeouts, and test configuration. Required for accessing application URL and environment-specific settings.
Test reporting and validation mechanism. Handles Allure report generation, screenshot attachments, and test result capture. Required for test reporting and validation tracking.
Pytest configuration and fixtures. Provides essential fixtures including reporter(), main_execution(), session_cleanup(), and test hooks. Required for pytest test execution framework.

test_case_resolutions.flow_pattern: Navigate to Login Page → Enter Username → Enter Password → Click Login Button → Verify Dashboard Displayed → Verify User Info → Verify Navigation Menu → Verify Logout Button → Click Logout → Verify Redirected to Login Page

components_to_create.name: LoginPage
components_to_create.description: Dedicated page object for standalone login page with locators for login form elements (username field, password field, login button, login form container, error message display) and methods for login interactions. The AURA framework does not have a dedicated login page object - authentication is handled through HPIDPage which is designed for HP ID account creation during enrollment, not standalone login testing.
components_to_create.reason: KB confirms no dedicated LoginPage exists. HPIDPage handles HP ID flows during enrollment, not standalone login. Test case requires a dedicated login page object for login testing.
components_to_create.kb_confirmation: KB query confirmed: 'The AURA framework does NOT have a dedicated LoginPage page object file. Login functionality is handled by pages/hpid_page.py with the HPIDPage class, which manages HP ID authentication (sign-in/sign-up).'

execution_order.description: Navigate to application login page using base URL from settings
Enter valid username into username input field
Enter valid password into password input field
Click login button to submit credentials and authenticate
Verify dashboard page is displayed after successful login
Get displayed username and verify it matches logged-in user

execution_order.component: pages/overview_page.py or pages/dashboard_side_menu_page.py → verify navigation menu visible
execution_order.description: Verify navigation menu is displayed on dashboard
Verify logout button is displayed and accessible
Click logout button to log out and navigate to login page
Verify user is redirected to login page after logout

missing_coverage_analysis.explanation: The AURA framework does not have components specifically designed for standalone login testing. All 10 test steps require new component creation. The framework's authentication is built around HP ID account creation during enrollment flows (HPIDHelper, HPIDPage) rather than standalone login/logout testing. While foundation infrastructure exists (browser management, base page objects, test setup), the specific login testing components (LoginPage, login methods, logout functionality, dashboard verification methods) do not exist and must be created.

test_case_resolutions.dependency_notes: All page objects depend on pages/base_page_object.py. DashboardHelper depends on overview_page.py, privacy_banner_page.py, dashboard_side_menu_page.py, and hpid_helper.py. HPIDHelper depends on hpid_page.py. Test execution requires foundation files: test_flows_common, playwright_manager, settings, allure_reporter, conftest.py.
test_case_resolutions.navigation_chain: Login Page → Dashboard/Overview Page → Login Page (after logout)

summary.framework_readiness: LOW - Significant component creation required for login testing
summary.recommendation: The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment and subscription management testing, not standalone login/logout testing. To automate this login test case, you must create: (1) A dedicated LoginPage page object with login form locators and methods, (2) Logout functionality (locator and method) in dashboard page objects, (3) Dashboard verification methods in OverviewPage for user info and navigation menu validation. Consider whether this test case aligns with the framework's intended use case (enrollment flows) or if it should be adapted to test login as part of an enrollment scenario where existing HPIDHelper.sign_in() could be leveraged.
